name: Chinese, Bachelor of Arts

requirements.heading: Preparatory Subject Matter
notes: Equivalent as determined by a required language placement exam.

requirements.heading: Required Courses
notes: Grading. Students may take up to two and no more than two (regular, letter-graded) courses for the major on a P/NP basis. Courses which are only offered on a P/NP basis do not count toward these limits.
Note: With prior approval of the undergraduate advisor, students already proficient in Chinese at any third-year level must take other upper division CHN courses to replace language course(s).
Recommended substitutions from outside the department:
Other advanced literature and culture courses selected in consultation with the undergraduate advisor.

advising_notes: Honors Program: To be eligible to receive high or highest honors in the Chinese major, students must complete a senior thesis project. A student interested in pursuing a senior thesis project must enroll in CHN 194H and complete a scholarly paper or similar research project under the direction of a senate faculty member. The thesis project will have a minimum duration of two quarters and carry a minimum of 6 units of credit. Up to 4 CHN 194H units earned can be counted toward the major requirements. To qualify
